questions.q: The enzyme pepsin is secreted by?
The wall of small intestine is made up of?
Digestion of proteins begins in which part of the digestive system?
questions.options: a) Mouth, b) Stomach, c) Small intestine, d) Large intestine

questions.q: Carbohydrates are broken down into which sugar in the small intestine?

questions.options: a) Amylase, b) Lipase, c) Trypsin, d) Pepsin

questions.q: Describe the process of absorption of fats in the small intestine. (10 marks)
questions.answer: The process of absorption of fats in the small intestine occurs through the formation of micelles. The bile salts emulsify the fats into smaller droplets, which increases their surface area. The lipase enzyme breaks down the fats into fatty acids and monoglycerides. The broken-down fats then dissolve in the micelles and are absorbed into the enterocytes. The micelles break down inside the enterocytes, and the fatty acids are re-esterified into triglycerides.

questions.q: Name the type of epithelium present in the lining of the small intestine.
What is the role of bile salts in the digestion of fats?
questions.answer: Bile salts emulsify fats into smaller droplets, increasing their surface area and allowing lipase to break them down into fatty acids and monoglycerides.

questions.q: The mucous membrane of the small intestine secretes?
Compare the structure and function of the small and large intestine. (10 marks)
questions.answer: The small intestine is a long, thin tube with finger-like projections called villi. The villi increase the surface area of the small intestine, allowing for efficient absorption of nutrients. The large intestine is a wider tube with a thicker wall, and its main function is to absorb water and electrolytes from the remaining waste material.
